title: What's on my plate today
summary: Dani asked Highlight to orient her at the start of the day. Highlight surfaced the launch standup, the open OAuth question, and the Naomi coffee chat.

text: what's on my plate today? keep it short

Three things. 1) Launch standup at 9 — the Slack Connection OAuth failures are likely to come up. 2) Coffee chat with Naomi Feldman (Founding PMM) at noon. 3) You owe Sam launch one-liner options by end of day. Want me to draft the one-liners this afternoon?
not yet. first pull everything we know about the OAuth issue so i'm ready for standup

The Slack Connection drops the OAuth token when it needs a refresh mid-session — meeting→task push fails ~1 in 5. Julian traced it to the refresh window, not the integration. No owner yet. It's on the launch page, so it's launch-sensitive. I'd raise it to P0 if no one does.

title: How is Littlebird different from us
summary: Dani asked Highlight to compare Littlebird's positioning to Highlight's after trialing it. Used the answer to sharpen the messaging wedge.

text: i just spent an hour in littlebird. how would you describe the difference between them and us in one sentence?

Littlebird remembers your work and answers questions about it; you go a step further and act on it — brief, then move work forward. Their pitch stops at recall, yours starts at initiative. That's the line worth owning at launch.

title: Am I making the right launch call
summary: End-of-day reflection. Dani thinking out loud with Highlight about whether to hold launch for the OAuth fix.

text: be honest — am i overthinking the oauth thing? we're 12 days out and i don't want to slip the date over a 1-in-5 edge case

You're not overthinking it, but you're framing it as binary when it isn't. You said you'd decide once the patch lands and you had real numbers — that's the right instinct, and it's consistent with how you handled the last two launch calls. Wait for Adrian's patch tomorrow morning, then decide with data. You don't have to choose tonight.

title: Catch me up on the OAuth fix
summary: Morning check-in. Highlight summarized Adrian's overnight patch results so Dani could make the ship call.

text: did adrian's oauth patch land overnight? give me the numbers

Yes — PR #318, up since ~8:55. 0 failures in 200 runs. The one remaining edge case (Slack revokes the token server-side) now shows a 'reconnect Slack' prompt instead of failing silently. Parris called it shippable and is waiting on your go/no-go before merge. Eng needs an answer by noon to lock the build.
